title: Delicious Springy Argentinian Feta Empanadas
description: A kid-friendly Argentinian dish featuring feta and a nimono-simmer, these empanadas are perfect for a fun and tasty snack. The combination of feta and springy dough creates a delicious umami flavor.
instructions: Combine flour, yeast, salt, and sugar in a large mixing bowl to create the empanada dough.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of feta cheese in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the empanadas cool for 5 minutes before serving.
